title: Andesanthus lepidotus
intro: Andesanthus lepidotus, commonly known as "Palo de la Candelaria", is a shrubby plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Andesanthus lepidotus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Andesanthus lepidotus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health ailments, including coughs, colds, and flu.
taxonomy: Andesanthus lepidotus is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It belongs to the genus Andesanthus, which is a group of plants native to the Andes mountain range. The plant is a shrub or a small tree with distinctive leaves. It is classified as a member of the order Malpighiales, which includes other plants with similar characteristics.
